domain: Diatom (Thalassiosira, Coscinodiscus) silica frustule hierarchical self-assembly — bottom-up nano-to-micro pore structure formed by cooperative silaffin/LCPA peptide self-assembly within the SDV.
specific_mechanism: Silicic acid → silica nanospheres (~20 nm) → cooperatively organized into mesh patterns (~100 nm) → assembled into layered frustule (~μm) via cooperative peptide binding objective inside the silica deposition vesicle.
llm_application: FRUSTULE-FT: hierarchical self-assembling LoRA adapters. Initialize many small rank-2 adapters in fine sub-layer subspaces. A cross-adapter cooperative binding objective ('peptide loss') encourages compatible small adapters to merge into mid-scale rank-8 clusters, which further merge into rank-32 layer modules. The final adapter hierarchy emerges bottom-up from data-driven cooperative merging rather than from a top-down rank schedule.
novelty_claim: HiLo (2502.03884) configures rank/count per-layer top-down. LoRA library (2405.11157) retrieves pre-built adapters. CompAs sums adapter parameters compositionally. None initiate small adapters and let them hierarchically self-assemble via cooperative binding objectives. Bottom-up emergent rank hierarchy is mechanistically distinct.
